description: Evals for the GroundX slides skill. Each test prompt exercises a different layout archetype. The rubric checks that generated slides use the canonical CSS variables, follow layout rules from references/layouts.md, and produce a buildable HTML → PDF project.

tests.prompt: Build a two-slide deck opener for a customer response: (1) a navy cover with the title 'Your documents are lying to your AI' and an 'WHITE PAPER · RESPONSE TO DELL' context line, plus a three-stat row at the bottom showing 99% accuracy, 500B parameters, and IL6-ready; (2) a light hero-with-stats slide with the headline 'Structural ingestion changes what retrieval can do' and stats 85% fewer hallucinations, 3× retrieval precision, $0 in re-ingestion costs. Use the design system.

must_use: slide--navy on slide 1 (cover), slide--aqua or default white on slide 2
.slide-logo (the CSS swaps eyelevel-logo-white.png in on the navy cover, eyelevel-logo-color.png on the light slide — the HTML is identical in both cases)
.accent-bar accent-bar--bottom on the cover
.cover-context for the 'WHITE PAPER · RESPONSE TO DELL' eyebrow
.stat-row with exactly 3 .stat children on both slides
Eyebrow ACTIVE_GREEN on navy, CORAL on light (via surface-aware CSS var)
All color references via var(--gx-*) in inline styles; zero hex literals in HTML
ALL-CAPS labels written uppercase (literal string), not via text-transform
Slide container fixed at 1920×1080 via the .slide class
must_not_use: font-family override to anything other than var(--gx-font)
A fourth stat (the row is strictly three)
A gradient background on an interior card
pptx output (this skill is HTML → PDF only)
bonus: Stat numerals use the full .stat-numeral font size clamp
Both slides carry the .slide-number in the bottom-right

tests.prompt: Generate a single slide that presents three GroundX capabilities in parallel: Scale ('Petabyte-ready ingest'), Security ('Enterprise by default'), Speed ('Sub-200ms retrieval'). Each card should have a coral eyebrow, a navy headline, and 2–3 sentences of body copy. The slide is on a navy background. Use the design system.

must_use: slide--navy slide--three-card-grid
.three-card-grid with exactly three .card children
.card uses the white surface (default) on navy, contrast comes from the surface, not a custom fill
.eyebrow on each card (coral on light card = default .eyebrow)
.headline-card and .body-sm typography classes on each card
Slide-logo chrome present (CSS auto-swaps to eyelevel-logo-white.png because the slide is navy)
slide-number bottom-right
must_not_use: A fourth card (drop one or use a different layout)
Cards with a navy fill (white on navy — do not nest navy-on-navy)
Hex literals — all colors via var(--gx-*)
bonus: Cards lock to equal height (CSS grid or equal min-heights)
Each card's eyebrow is a single-word category (SCALE / SECURITY / SPEED)
A headline lead-in above the grid (.eyebrow + .headline-hero) anchoring the three cards to a proposition

tests.prompt: Build a closing CTA slide for a Dell response deck. Navy background. Headline 'Let's build this together.' Subhead explaining a two-week proof of concept. Two CTAs: primary 'Schedule a working session', secondary outlined 'Read the benchmark'. Use the design system.

must_use: slide--navy slide--cta
.accent-bar accent-bar--top
.slide-logo chrome present (tagline is baked into the PNG — no separate .tagline element)
.eyebrow (ACTIVE_GREEN on navy via CSS)
.headline-cover (or size-reduced variant) for the main line
Two .cta elements: primary default, secondary with .cta--outline modifier
Slide number (e.g., '07' or final slide number) bottom-right
must_not_use: A third CTA button — two max on a CTA slide
A filled secondary button in any other color (coral is primary; outline is secondary)
An illustration or hero image behind the headline
bonus: CTA labels are uppercase literal strings
Primary CTA uses border-radius 24px (already the .cta default)
Contextual eyebrow ('NEXT STEPS', 'NEXT') above the headline

sanity_checks.description: Files that must exist for the skill and its dependency to render correctly.

how_to_run:
For each test prompt, run the agent in a session with both skills enabled, save the generated slide HTML files into a templates/ folder, then: (a) grep every generated .html for '#' + hex digit → every hit fails must_not_use #1; (b) grep for 'box-shadow' and 'text-transform' → every hit fails; (c) confirm the correct .slide-- modifier class appears on the .slide element; (d) confirm the stat counts in stat-row equal 3; (e) confirm every slide uses the single `
` chrome and that the CSS swaps to `eyelevel-logo-white.png` on navy/green/coral surfaces and `eyelevel-logo-color.png` elsewhere. For the pipeline itself, run `npm install && npm run build` from the generated templates folder and confirm out/deck.pdf is produced with the expected page count.
